summary: Iteration 4 backend-only tests for Probexa's rewritten A+ logic, full-market scan, watchlist details enrichment, advanced-analysis toggles (liq_sweep + hi_tf_confirm), new prefs.advanced field, and login/session upsert stability. Created /app/backend/tests/test_iteration4_scan_setup.py with 15 targeted tests across 6 test classes. Result: 15/15 PASSED (final run). Verified: (a) /api/setup/{symbol} returns display_checklist (6 keys), liquidity_sweep_status, htf_status; grade/action fully consistent with checklist pass count and direction for BTC/ETH/SOL. (b) hi_tf_confirm=0 correctly yields htf_status=null; hi_tf_confirm=1 yields confirmed|unconfirmed|null; liq_sweep=1 yields possible_sweep|real_breakout|null. (c) /api/scan returns universe_size ~200-300 and scanned_count >>20; every A+ in best_setups has all 6 checklist true and action BUY/SELL with score>=90. (d) hi_tf_confirm=1 propagates htf_status through every scan item. (e) /api/watchlist/details enriches BTCUSDT with real price + all 8 required fields. (f) prefs.advanced (liquidity_sweep_detection, higher_timeframe_confirmation) persists via PUT and returned in GET; partial updates merge correctly. (g) POST /api/auth/session with bogus session_id returns 401 cleanly (no 500 / duplicate-key crash) — upsert fix confirmed. One transient flake observed on first full run (BTCUSDT price=None in watchlist/details due to OKX kline fetch race with concurrent scan) but self-resolved on retry once cache warmed.

minor.issue: If OKX klines fetch inside _fetch_one_setup fails/times out (e.g. rate-limit during concurrent scan warmup), the endpoint silently returns price=null, action='WAIT', trade_grade='-'. Consider a 1x internal retry or fallback to ticker price for at least the `price` field so the UI never shows a blank price for a symbol OKX otherwise serves.

action_items: (Optional) Add a single retry (or fallback to /api/market/ticker) in watchlist_details when _fetch_one_setup returns None so transient OKX slowness never surfaces as price=null in the UI.
(Refactor, non-blocking) server.py is now 1069 lines — split into routers (auth, market, scan, watchlist, alerts, journal, prefs, calculator) for maintainability. Same suggestion as iteration 3.
critical_code_review_comments: scan_engine.build_setup L297-303: advanced-analysis demotions (htf_status=='unconfirmed' → A, liquidity_sweep_status=='possible_sweep' → B/WAIT) are only applied AFTER the direction-based grade block, which is correct, but the /api/setup endpoint only computes htf_status/liq_sweep when the respective query flag is set — meaning the SAME symbol at the SAME timestamp will grade differently depending on whether the caller passes hi_tf_confirm=1 or not. This is by design (opt-in advanced) but worth documenting.

server.py L346-348: when liq_sweep=0 the scan response explicitly overwrites liquidity_sweep_status to None on every item. This is fine, but note that build_setup may have already applied a liq_sweep demotion inside the grade block (L299-303 of scan_engine) BEFORE that field is masked out — so the caller sees no reason for a symbol being demoted. If the intent is 'liq_sweep flag off = no detection at all', consider gating the demotion logic on the caller flag inside build_setup (pass through a bool) rather than masking the status field post-hoc.

server.py L328: scan cache key includes hi_tf_confirm but NOT liq_sweep, yet the response body varies with liq_sweep (status field masked/unmasked). Two callers on the same TF with different liq_sweep flags will receive the wrong cached payload for ~30s. Add liq_sweep to the cache key.

server.py L84-85: user_sessions unique index on session_token exists, but the fix relies on update_one({session_token:..}, {$set:...}, upsert=True). Confirmed working (invalid session correctly returns 401, no duplicate-key on retry). Good.

server.py L602-638 watchlist_details: when _fetch_one_setup returns None (OKX flake), price is emitted as null. Prefer falling back to /api/market/ticker price so the enrichment endpoint stays visually consistent.

success_rate.backend: 100% (15/15) on final run; 14/15 on cold-cache first run (flake resolved on retry)

seed_data_creation: Tests idempotently add BTCUSDT to user_test001's watchlist and toggle prefs.advanced. No teardown required — mutations are order-tolerant.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 4 verified: A+ grade/action is now deterministically tied to display_checklist (6/6 → A+, 5/6 → A, ≤4/6 → WAIT/B/C), rooted in scan_engine.build_setup L264-303. /api/scan returns universe_size>100 and scanned_count>20 (OKX perps universe cached for TTL). /api/watchlist/details enriches with 8 fields (symbol, price, change_pct, action, trade_grade, ai_score, confidence, direction) — but silently returns nulls if OKX klines fetch fails; consider retry. /api/prefs now includes `advanced` dict with liquidity_sweep_detection & higher_timeframe_confirmation booleans, merged partially on PUT. /api/auth/session invalid session_id → 401 (upsert fix good). Bearer token: test-token-abc-123 for user_test001. Combined with iterations 1-3, total backend surface tested = 43 + 15 = 58 test cases.
rca_of_issue: Single flaky failure on first cold-cache run: TestWatchlistDetails::test_watchlist_details_enriched saw price=None for BTCUSDT. Root cause: the /api/watchlist/details call fired ~1-2s after the module-scoped scan_payload fixture triggered a full-universe scan (~250 concurrent kline requests), and OKX klines for BTCUSDT briefly rate-limited or timed out inside _fetch_one_setup (which then returns None → fallback dict with price=null in server.py L628-637). Reproduction: run pytest with no cache. Mitigation: (1) retry once inside _fetch_one_setup on kline fetch failure, or (2) fallback to /api/market/ticker for at least the price field in watchlist_details. Test passes reliably on cache-warm runs, so we did NOT modify production code — only reported as a minor issue.
